page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC Milan architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 188.0 GB of RAM, yielding approximately 3.91 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 960 GB SSD, and the instance operates under a QEMU hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and single-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 73650.13 and single-core scoring 3058.9, both placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read bandwidth is strong at 3511.81 GB/sec, while large-block uncached read bandwidth is average at 154.31 GB/sec. The server demonstrates high efficiency in database operations, scoring 22243.57 on PassMark database tests, and achieves 64.55 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing tasks also perform in the top 25%.

Given the dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, the ccx63 is appropriate for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database hosting, and medium-scale LLM text generation. While it achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models, performance drops to average levels for larger 70B parameter models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. The server offers high resource density and dedicated compute capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for continuous, CPU-bound production workloads that require predictable performance without burstable limitations.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) powered by the AMD EPYC Milan x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 188.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 960 GB local SSD, and runs on the QEMU hypervisor without GPU accelerators. The server delivers strong multi-core compute performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and PassMark CPU benchmarks, alongside high database efficiency with 64.55 million Redis SET operations per second. While memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly performant, large uncached blocks exhibit average speeds. This hardware profile provides a balanced, dedicated resource pool suitable for demanding multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and small-to-medium language model inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188 GB of RAM, and a 960 GB local SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and compilation.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server with 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server offering balanced compute and memory resources with local SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with hyper-threading
188 GB of system memory and a bundled 960 GB local SSD
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and ray tracing
High-throughput database capabilities with 64.55 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for dedicated, non-burstable general-purpose and compute workloads
